   213→   213→
   214→   214→/// Filter out null-input tool_use blocks from MaxTokens truncation.
   215→   215→/// Returns the filtered blocks. If all tool_use blocks had null input
   216→   216→/// and nothing remains, returns a vec with a placeholder text block.
   217→   217→fn filter_null_input_tool_use(blocks: Vec<ContentBlock>) -> Vec<ContentBlock> {
   218→   218→    let filtered: Vec<ContentBlock> = blocks
   219→   219→        .into_iter()
   220→   220→        .filter(|b| {
   221→   221→            if let ContentBlock::ToolUse { input, .. } = b {
   222→   222→                !input.is_null()
   223→   223→            } else {
   224→   224→                true
   225→   225→            }
   226→   226→        })
   227→   227→        .collect();
   228→   228→
   229→   229→    if filtered.is_empty() {
   230→   230→        vec![ContentBlock::Text {
   231→   231→            text: "[Response truncated]".to_string(),
   232→   232→        }]
   233→   233→    } else {
   234→   234→        filtered
   235→   235→    }
   236→   236→}
   237→   237→
   238→   238→/// Decision from pre-dispatch protocol: allow dispatch, block with a ToolResult, or trip threshold.
   239→   239→#[derive(Debug)]
   240→   240→enum PreDispatchResult {
   241→   241→    Allow,
   242→   242→    Blocked(ContentBlock),
   243→   243→    ThresholdTripped,
   244→   244→}
   245→   245→
   246→   246→/// Unified pre-dispatch protocol for both parallel and sequential paths.
   247→   247→/// Checks null-input, runs pre-hook, manages block counting and threshold checks.
   248→   248→/// Block counts are mutated in place so callers cannot forget to apply them.
   249→   249→async fn run_pre_dispatch(
   250→   250→    hooks: &HookRunner,
   251→   251→    id: &str,
   252→   252→    name: &str,
   253→   253→    input: &serde_json::Value,
   254→   254→    iterations: usize,
   255→   255→    consecutive_block_count: &mut usize,
   256→   256→    total_block_count: &mut usize,
   257→   257→) -> PreDispatchResult {
   258→   258→    // Null-input safety net (truncated tool_use from API anomaly)
   259→   259→    if input.is_null() {
   260→   260→        return PreDispatchResult::Blocked(ContentBlock::ToolResult {
   261→   261→            tool_use_id: id.to_string(),
   262→   262→            content: "null input (truncated tool_use)".to_string(),
   263→   263→            is_error: Some(true),
   264→   264→        });
   265→   265→    }
   266→   266→
   267→   267→    let pre_result = hooks.run_pre_tool_use(name, input, iterations).await;
   268→   268→
   269→   269→    match pre_result {
   270→   270→        PreToolResult::Block { reason, .. } => {
   271→   271→            *consecutive_block_count += 1;
   272→   272→            *total_block_count += 1;
   273→   273→
   274→   274→            if *consecutive_block_count >= MAX_CONSECUTIVE_BLOCKS {
   275→   275→                eprintln!("[hooks] Consecutive block limit ({MAX_CONSECUTIVE_BLOCKS}) reached");
   276→   276→                return PreDispatchResult::ThresholdTripped;
   277→   277→            }
   278→   278→            if *total_block_count >= MAX_TOTAL_BLOCKS {
   279→   279→                eprintln!("[hooks] Total block limit ({MAX_TOTAL_BLOCKS}) reached");
   280→   280→                return PreDispatchResult::ThresholdTripped;
   281→   281→            }
   282→   282→
   283→   283→            PreDispatchResult::Blocked(ContentBlock::ToolResult {
   284→   284→                tool_use_id: id.to_string(),
   285→   285→                content: reason,
   286→   286→                is_error: Some(true),
   287→   287→            })
   288→   288→        }
   289→   289→        PreToolResult::Allow => {
   290→   290→            *consecutive_block_count = 0;
   291→   291→            PreDispatchResult::Allow
   292→   292→        }
   293→   293→    }
   294→   294→}
   295→   295→
   296→   296→/// Unified post-dispatch protocol for both parallel and sequential paths.
   297→   297→/// Formats and logs the result, then runs post-hooks. Returns true if a
   298→   298→/// convergence signal was received (caller should set signal_break).
   299→   299→async fn run_post_dispatch(
   300→   300→    hooks: &HookRunner,
   301→   301→    name: &str,
   302→   302→    input: &serde_json::Value,
   303→   303→    content: &str,
   304→   304→    is_error: bool,
   305→   305→    iterations: usize,
   306→   306→    verbose: bool,
   307→   307→) -> bool {
   308→   308→    let display = format_tool_result_display(content, is_error, verbose);
   309→   309→    eprintln!("{display}");
   310→   310→
   311→   311→    let post_result = hooks
   312→   312→        .run_post_tool_use(name, input, content, is_error, iterations)
   313→   313→        .await;
   314→   314→    matches!(post_result, PostToolResult::Signal { .. })
   315→   315→}
   316→   316→
   317→   317→fn log_tool_dispatch(name: &str, input: &serde_json::Value, verbose: bool) {
   318→   318→    if verbose {
   319→   319→        eprintln!("\n[tool] {name}({})", truncate_json(input, 100));
   320→   320→    } else {
   321→   321→        eprintln!("\n[tool] {name}");
   322→   322→    }
   323→   323→}
   324→   324→
   325→   325→fn threshold_reason_str(consecutive_block_count: usize) -> &'static str {
   326→   326→    if consecutive_block_count >= MAX_CONSECUTIVE_BLOCKS {
   327→   327→        "block_limit_consecutive"
   328→   328→    } else {
   329→   329→        "block_limit_total"
   330→   330→    }
   331→   331→}
   332→   332→
   333→   333→/// Dispatch a tool and wrap the result as a ContentBlock::ToolResult.
   334→   334→/// Used by the parallel path inside spawn_blocking.
   335→   335→fn dispatch_to_tool_result(id: String, name: String, input: serde_json::Value) -> ContentBlock {
   336→   336→    let result = dispatch_tool(&name, &input, &mut |_: &str| {});
   337→   337→    let (content, is_error) = match result {
   338→   338→        Ok(output) => (output, false),
   339→   339→        Err(err) => (err, true),
   340→   340→    };
   341→   341→    ContentBlock::ToolResult {
   342→   342→        tool_use_id: id,
   343→   343→        content,
   344→   344→        is_error: if is_error { Some(true) } else { None },
   345→   345→    }
   346→   346→}
   347→   347→
   348→   348→/// Join spawned parallel futures into their slots, handling panics.
   349→   349→async fn join_spawned_futures(
   350→   350→    futures: Vec<(usize, tokio::task::JoinHandle<ContentBlock>)>,
   351→   351→    slots: &mut [Option<ContentBlock>],
   352→   352→    tool_ids: &[(String, String, serde_json::Value)],
   353→   353→) {
   354→   354→    let handles: Vec<_> = futures
   355→   355→        .into_iter()
   356→   356→        .map(|(idx, h)| async move { (idx, h.await) })
   357→   357→        .collect();
   358→   358→    for (idx, result) in futures_util::future::join_all(handles).await {
   359→   359→        slots[idx] = Some(match result {
   360→   360→            Ok(block) => block,
   361→   361→            Err(_) => ContentBlock::ToolResult {
   362→   362→                tool_use_id: tool_ids[idx].0.clone(),
   363→   363→                content: "tool panicked".to_string(),
   364→   364→                is_error: Some(true),
   365→   365→            },
   366→   366→        });
   367→   367→    }
   368→   368→}
   369→   369→
   370→   370→fn format_tool_result_display(result: &str, is_error: bool, verbose: bool) -> String {
   371→   371→    if is_error {
   372→   372→        let preview = if result.len() > 200 {
   373→   373→            format!("{}...", &result[..result.floor_char_boundary(200)])
   374→   374→        } else {
   375→   375→            result.to_string()
   376→   376→        };
   377→   377→        format!("  Error: {preview}")
   378→   378→    } else if verbose {
   379→   379→        result.to_string()
   380→   380→    } else {
   381→   381→        format!("  ({} chars)", result.len())
   382→   382→    }
   383→   383→}

   873→   873→fn truncate_json(value: &serde_json::Value, max_len: usize) -> String {
   874→   874→    let s = value.to_string();
   875→   875→    if s.len() <= max_len {
   876→   876→        s
   877→   877→    } else {
   878→   878→        format!("{}...", &s[..s.floor_char_boundary(max_len)])
   879→   879→    }
   880→   880→}
   881→   881→
   882→   882→const MAX_CONTINUATIONS: usize = 3;
   883→   883→
   884→   884→/// Determine what to do after a MaxTokens stop_reason.
   885→   885→/// Returns the action to take given the filtered content blocks and current continuation count.
   886→   886→#[derive(Debug, PartialEq)]
   887→   887→enum MaxTokensAction {
   888→   888→    /// Response was empty (only placeholder) — break immediately
   889→   889→    BreakEmpty,
   890→   890→    /// Valid tool_use blocks present — fall through to tool dispatch
   891→   891→    DispatchTools,
   892→   892→    /// Text-only, under cap — inject continuation prompt
   893→   893→    Continue,
   894→   894→    /// Text-only, cap reached — break
   895→   895→    BreakCapReached,
   896→   896→}
   897→   897→
   898→   898→fn classify_max_tokens(blocks: &[ContentBlock], continuation_count: usize) -> MaxTokensAction {
   899→   899→    // Check for empty response (only the "[Response truncated]" placeholder)
   900→   900→    let is_empty = blocks.len() == 1
   901→   901→        && matches!(&blocks[0], ContentBlock::Text { text } if text == "[Response truncated]");
   902→   902→    if is_empty {
   903→   903→        return MaxTokensAction::BreakEmpty;
   904→   904→    }
   905→   905→
   906→   906→    let has_valid_tools = blocks
   907→   907→        .iter()
   908→   908→        .any(|b| matches!(b, ContentBlock::ToolUse { .. }));
   909→   909→
   910→   910→    if has_valid_tools {
   911→   911→        MaxTokensAction::DispatchTools
   912→   912→    } else if continuation_count < MAX_CONTINUATIONS {
   913→   913→        MaxTokensAction::Continue
   914→   914→    } else {
   915→   915→        MaxTokensAction::BreakCapReached
   916→   916→    }
   917→   917→}

   919→   919→/// Check if stdin is a terminal (not piped).
   920→   920→fn atty_check() -> bool {
   921→   921→    std::io::IsTerminal::is_terminal(&io::stdin())
   922→   922→}

/// Maximum output size for bash commands (1MB). Commands that exceed this are
/// killed to prevent unbounded memory growth from runaway processes.
const BASH_OUTPUT_LIMIT: usize = 1_048_576;

fn bash_exec(input: &Value, stream_cb: &mut dyn FnMut(&str)) -> Result<String, String> {
    let command = input["command"]
        .as_str()
        .ok_or("Missing required parameter: command")?;

    if is_denied_command(command) {
        return Err(format!("Command blocked by safety guard: {command}"));
    }

    let timeout = Duration::from_secs(120);
    let deadline = Instant::now() + timeout;

    let mut child = Command::new("bash")
        .arg("-c")
        .arg(command)
        .stdout(Stdio::piped())
        .stderr(Stdio::piped())
        .spawn()
        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to spawn bash: {e}"))?;

    let stdout = child.stdout.take().unwrap();
    let stderr = child.stderr.take().unwrap();

    let (tx_out, rx) = mpsc::channel::<String>();
    let tx_err = tx_out.clone();

    // Stdout reader thread
    std::thread::spawn(move || {

         and running commands.\n\n\
         Environment:\n\
         - Working directory: {cwd}\n\
         - Platform: {platform}\n\n\
         Available tools (use PascalCase names exactly):\n\
         - Read: Read file contents (max 1MB)\n\
         - Glob: List files matching a pattern (max 1000 entries)\n\

         - Edit: Edit files with exact text replacement (max 100KB, use replace_all for bulk)\n\

         - Read files before editing them\n\

{"parentUuid":"411d21ca-bd30-4bdd-a1b7-618377310f7a","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"a139a8f1-461e-470a-a74c-542f892f5706","agentId":"a500136c93a86ecb2","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→---
     2→status: Complete
     3→created: 2026-02-14
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Unified Rust Coding Agent Specification
     7→
     8→**Target:** Single binary, streaming, subagent-aware, <950 production lines
     9→**Pin:** Go source at `/reference/go-source/` — pattern-match against working code
    10→
    11→---
    12→
    13→## Requirements
    14→
    15→**R1. Core Loop**
    16→- REPL: read user input → call Claude API → dispatch tools → repeat
    17→- Streaming responses (SSE, not batch) from day 1
    18→- Conversation history management (add user input, add assistant message to context array)
    19→
    20→**R2. HTTP Client**
    21→- Build own with `reqwest` (not third-party Anthropic crate)
    22→- Anthropic API: POST to `/v1/messages`
    23→- Handle SSE for streaming responses
    24→- Expose `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env var
    25→
    26→**R3. Tool Registry Pattern**
    27→```rust
    28→// tools! macro generates all_tool_schemas() from one definition
    29→tools! {
    30→    "read_file", "Read a file", schema;
    31→    "list_files", "List files", schema;
    32→    // ...
    33→}
    34→// dispatch_tool() is hand-written — bash gets streaming callback, others don't
    35→```
    36→- 5 tools total; each follows Anthropic tool_use spec
    37→- Tool schemas are sent with every API request, providing introspection natively
    38→- Macro generates schemas; dispatch is hand-written to support per-tool signatures (bash streaming)
    39→
    40→**R4. Five Tools**
    41→1. **Read** — Read(path) → file contents (handle binary, size limits)
    42→2. **Glob** — Glob(path?, recursive?) → [files]
    43→3. **Bash** — Bash(command, cwd?) → stdout/stderr (timeout, streaming output via callback)
    44→4. **Edit** — Edit(path, old_str, new_str, replace_all?) → success/error (exact match by default; replace_all=true for bulk changes; empty old_str on missing file = create with mkdir, empty old_str on existing file = append)
    45→5. **Grep** — Grep(pattern, path?, file_type?, case_sensitive?) → matches (shell out to `rg`)
    46→
    47→**R5. CLI Interface**
    48→- Single binary, no subcommands required
    49→- `--verbose` flag for debug output
    50→- `--model` flag (default: claude-opus-4-6)
    51→- `--max-tokens` flag (default: 16384, API supports up to 128K)
    52→- Read prompts/context from stdin if available, interactive prompt otherwise
    53→- Piped stdin read as single prompt (not line-by-line)
    54→- Exit gracefully on EOF or "exit" command
    55→
    56→**R6. Error Handling**
    57→- `thiserror` for structured error types in api.rs (`AgentError`)
    58→- Tool errors returned as `Result<String, String>` — raw strings flow into tool_result text
    59→- Display errors to user, continue loop (don't panic)
    60→
    61→**R7. Streaming Architecture**
    62→- Collect SSE events into a response buffer
    63→- Check `stop_reason` for "tool_use" vs "end_turn"
    64→- If tool_use: extract tool calls, execute, send results back
    65→- If end_turn: display response to user, prompt for next input
    66→
    67→**R8. Subagent Awareness (Deferred)**
    68→- Explicitly deferred per non-goals; no SubagentContext types in codebase
    69→- StopReason enum is the only surviving artifact (used for tool dispatch loop control)
    70→- Future subagent dispatch would add a StopReason variant or context field
    71→
    72→---
    73→
    74→## Architecture
    75→
    76→```
    77→src/
    78→  main.rs         — CLI, loop, error handling
    79→  api.rs          — Anthropic client (reqwest + SSE)
    80→  tools/
    81→    mod.rs        — All 5 tools with tools! macro (read, list, bash, edit, search)
    82→
    83→reference/
    84→  go-source/      — Cloned Go workshop code (pin)
    85→```
    86→
    87→---
    88→
    89→## Success Criteria
    90→
    91→- [ ] Binary compiles (`cargo build`)
    92→- [ ] Tests pass (`cargo test`)
    93→- [ ] Clippy clean (`cargo clippy -- -D warnings`)
    94→- [ ] Formatted (`cargo fmt --check`)
    95→- [ ] Can chat with Claude
    96→- [ ] Can read files
    97→- [ ] Can list directories
    98→- [ ] Can run bash commands
    99→- [ ] Can edit files (exact-match semantics)
   100→- [ ] Can search code
   101→- [ ] <950 production lines
   102→- [ ] Streaming responses visible to user in real-time
   103→
   104→---
   105→
   106→## Dependencies
   107→
   108→- `reqwest` — HTTP client (with stream feature)
   109→- `serde` + `serde_json` — JSON serialization
   110→- `tokio` — async runtime (full features)
   111→- `clap` — CLI parsing (derive feature)
   112→- `thiserror` — error types
   113→- `futures-util` — stream consumption for SSE parsing
   114→
   115→---
   116→
   117→## Non-Goals
   118→
   119→- Progressive binaries (Phases 1-3 merged into single unified agent)
   120→- Batch mode (streaming from day 1)
   121→- Provider abstraction (Anthropic only)
   122→- Interactive line editing (simple readline via stdin)
   123→- Subagent execution (StopReason enum retained for loop control, SubagentContext removed)
   124→
   125→---
   126→
   127→## Implementation Notes
   128→
   129→- `edit_file` uses exact single-match by default; `replace_all=true` replaces every occurrence (for renames, bulk changes)
   130→- Tool dispatch is synchronous; async only for HTTP and command execution
   131→- Context accumulates in memory; no persistence layer. Conversation trimmed at exchange boundaries (720KB budget, ~180K tokens) to prevent unbounded growth while preserving tool_use/tool_result pairing
   132→- No automatic retry; failures return to user for decision
   133→- Search tool shells out to `rg` (must be installed)
   134→- Dynamic system prompt: `build_system_prompt()` injects cwd, platform, structured tool guidance, and safety rules at startup
   135→- reqwest client timeouts: 30s connect, 300s request (prevents indefinite hangs)
   136→- Bash command guard: deny-list blocks destructive patterns (rm -rf /, rm -fr /, fork bombs, dd to devices, mkfs, chmod 777 /, git push --force, git push -f) before shell execution, including reversed flag order variants. Commands are whitespace-normalized (lowercase + collapse spaces/tabs) before matching to catch bypass via extra whitespace
   137→- NO_COLOR convention: all ANSI output suppressed when `NO_COLOR` env var is set
   138→- API error recovery: pop trailing User message + orphaned tool_use to maintain conversation alternation invariant
   139→- Tool loop safety: 50-iteration limit prevents runaway agent behavior; calls recover_conversation on break to maintain alternation invariant
   140→- Tool result visibility: non-verbose mode shows result size (chars); errors always shown with 200-char preview (matches Go reference pattern of always showing tool results)
   141→- Tool schema descriptions enriched with limits (1MB, 100KB, 1000 entries, 50 matches, 120s timeout) so the model sees constraints in both schema and system prompt
   142→- Retry-After header surfaced on 429 rate limit responses for better user-facing diagnostics
   143→- code_search surfaces actionable error when rg (ripgrep) is not installed instead of cryptic "No such file" OS error
   144→- Bash streaming: channel-based output streaming via mpsc channels + 50ms polling loop. Reader threads send 4KB chunks, polling loop drains and forwards to caller callback. Partial output preserved on timeout. Eliminates wait-timeout dependency (replaced by try_wait + Instant deadline)
   145→- edit_file replace_all: optional boolean parameter for bulk replacements. Default false preserves single-match safety. Error message hints at replace_all when duplicates found
   146→
   147→---
   148→
   149→## Reference: Go Source
   150→
   151→The Go workshop (`reference/go-source/`) contains 6 progressive versions:
   152→- `chat.go` — bare event loop
   153→- `read.go` — +read_file tool
   154→- `list_files.go` — +list_files tool
   155→- `bash_tool.go` — +bash tool
   156→- `edit_tool.go` — +edit_file tool
   157→- `code_search_tool.go` — +code_search tool
   158→
   159→Study the event loop in `edit_tool.go` (lines 126-214) as the canonical loop pattern. The Rust implementation should follow the same structure: API call → check response → dispatch tools → send results → repeat.
   160→"}]},"uuid":"38abe01d-5ad0-4f44-9ba8-02b266760616","timestamp":"2026-03-12T04:45:32.504Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"411d21ca-bd30-4bdd-a1b7-618377310f7a","userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/basher8383/3I/forge/forgeflare-hooks","sessionId":"8aac52da-2edf-4887-bae8-ee91f66f8d8c","version":"2.1.74","gitBranch":"main","slug":"peaceful-plotting-wand"}

   121→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq)]
   122→pub enum ToolEffect {
   123→    Pure,
   124→    Mutating,
   125→}
   126→
   127→pub fn tool_effect(name: &str) -> ToolEffect {
   128→    match name {
   129→        "Read" | "Glob" | "Grep" => ToolEffect::Pure,
   130→        "Bash" | "Edit" => ToolEffect::Mutating,
   131→        _ => ToolEffect::Mutating,
   132→    }
   133→}
   134→
   135→/// Dispatch a tool call by name. Returns Ok(output) or Err(error_message).
   136→/// Bash gets a streaming callback; other tools don't need one.
   137→pub fn dispatch_tool(
   138→    name: &str,
   139→    input: &Value,
   140→    stream_cb: &mut dyn FnMut(&str),
   141→) -> Result<String, String> {
   142→    match name {
   143→        "Read" => read_exec(input),
   144→        "Glob" => glob_exec(input),
   145→        "Bash" => bash_exec(input, stream_cb),
   146→        "Edit" => edit_exec(input),
   147→        "Grep" => grep_exec(input),
   148→        _ => Err(format!("Unknown tool: {name}")),
   149→    }
   150→}
   151→
   152→fn read_exec(input: &Value) -> Result<String, String> {
   153→    let file_path = input["file_path"]
   154→        .as_str()
   155→        .ok_or("Missing required parameter: file_path")?;
   156→
   157→    let path = Path::new(file_path);
   158→    if !path.exists() {
   159→        return Err(format!("File not found: {file_path}"));
   160→    }
   161→
   162→    let metadata =
   163→        std::fs::metadata(path).map_err(|e| format!("Cannot read file metadata: {e}"))?;
   164→    if metadata.len() > 1_048_576 {
   165→        return Err(format!(
   166→            "File too large: {} bytes (limit: 1MB)",
   167→            metadata.len()
   168→        ));
   169→    }
   170→
   171→    let content = std::fs::read(path).map_err(|e| format!("Cannot read file: {e}"))?;
   172→
   173→    // Check for binary content (NUL bytes in first 8KB)
   174→    let check_len = content.len().min(8192);
   175→    if content[..check_len].contains(&0) {
   176→        return Ok(format!(
   177→            "[Binary file: {file_path}, {} bytes]",
   178→            content.len()
   179→        ));
   180→    }
   181→
   182→    String::from_utf8(content).map_err(|_| format!("File contains invalid UTF-8: {file_path}"))
   183→}
   184→
   185→fn glob_exec(input: &Value) -> Result<String, String> {
   186→    let pattern = input["pattern"]
   187→        .as_str()
   188→        .ok_or("Missing required parameter: pattern")?;
   189→    let base = input["path"].as_str().unwrap_or(".");
   190→
   191→    let full_pattern = if pattern.starts_with('/') || pattern.starts_with('.') {
   192→        pattern.to_string()
   193→    } else {
   194→        format!("{base}/{pattern}")
   195→    };
   196→
   197→    let expanded = expand_braces(&full_pattern);
   198→
   199→    let mut seen = HashSet::new();
   200→    let mut results = Vec::new();
   201→    const LIMIT: usize = 1000;
   202→
   203→    for pat in &expanded {
   204→        let paths =
   205→            glob::glob(pat).map_err(|e| format!("Invalid glob pattern '{}': {}", pat, e))?;
   206→        for entry in paths {
   207→            if results.len() >= LIMIT {
   208→                break;
   209→            }
   210→            // Skip entries with errors (permission denied, etc.)
   211→            let path = match entry {
   212→                Ok(p) => p,
   213→                Err(_) => continue,
   214→            };
   215→            let path_str = path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
   216→            if seen.insert(path_str.clone()) {
   217→                results.push(path_str);
   218→            }
   219→        }
   220→        if results.len() >= LIMIT {
   221→            break;
   222→        }
   223→    }
   224→
   225→    if results.is_empty() {
   226→        Ok("No files found".to_string())
   227→    } else {
   228→        Ok(results.join("\n"))
   229→    }
   230→}
   231→
   232→/// Expand a single top-level brace group in a glob pattern.
   233→/// `**/*.{rs,toml}` → `["**/*.rs", "**/*.toml"]`
   234→/// `src/{main,lib}.rs` → `["src/main.rs", "src/lib.rs"]`
   235→/// No braces or unmatched braces → returns the original pattern unchanged.
   236→fn expand_braces(pattern: &str) -> Vec<String> {
   237→    let open = match pattern.find('{') {
   238→        Some(i) => i,
   239→        None => return vec![pattern.to_string()],
   240→    };
   241→    let close = match pattern[open..].find('}') {
   242→        Some(i) => open + i,
   243→        None => return vec![pattern.to_string()],
   244→    };
   245→
   246→    let prefix = &pattern[..open];
   247→    let suffix = &pattern[close + 1..];
   248→    let alternatives = &pattern[open + 1..close];
   249→
   250→    alternatives
   251→        .split(',')
   252→        .map(|alt| format!("{prefix}{alt}{suffix}"))
   253→        .collect()
   254→}
   255→
   256→/// Deny-list patterns for bash commands. Whitespace-normalized lowercase matching.
   257→const BASH_DENY_LIST: &[&str] = &[
   258→    "rm -rf /",
   259→    "rm -fr /",
   260→    "rm -rf /*",
   261→    "rm -fr /*",
   262→    ":(){ :|:& };:",
   263→    "dd if=/dev",
   264→    "mkfs",
   265→    "chmod 777 /",
   266→    "git push --force",
   267→    "git push -f",
   268→];
   269→
   270→fn normalize_command(cmd: &str) -> String {
   271→    cmd.to_lowercase()
   272→        .split_whitespace()
   273→        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
   274→        .join(" ")
   275→}
   276→
   277→fn is_denied_command(cmd: &str) -> bool {
   278→    let normalized = normalize_command(cmd);
   279→    BASH_DENY_LIST
   280→        .iter()
   281→        .any(|pattern| normalized.contains(pattern))
   282→}
   283→
   284→/// Maximum output size for bash commands (1MB). Commands that exceed this are
   285→/// killed to prevent unbounded memory growth from runaway processes.
   286→const BASH_OUTPUT_LIMIT: usize = 1_048_576;
   287→
   288→fn bash_exec(input: &Value, stream_cb: &mut dyn FnMut(&str)) -> Result<String, String> {
   289→    let command = input["command"]
   290→        .as_str()
   291→        .ok_or("Missing required parameter: command")?;
   292→
   293→    if is_denied_command(command) {
   294→        return Err(format!("Command blocked by safety guard: {command}"));
   295→    }
   296→
   297→    let timeout = Duration::from_secs(120);
   298→    let deadline = Instant::now() + timeout;
   299→
   300→    let mut child = Command::new("bash")
   301→        .arg("-c")
   302→        .arg(command)
   303→        .stdout(Stdio::piped())
   304→        .stderr(Stdio::piped())
   305→        .spawn()
   306→        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to spawn bash: {e}"))?;
   307→
   308→    let stdout = child.stdout.take().unwrap();
   309→    let stderr = child.stderr.take().unwrap();
   310→
   311→    let (tx_out, rx) = mpsc::channel::<String>();
   312→    let tx_err = tx_out.clone();
   313→
   314→    // Stdout reader thread
   315→    std::thread::spawn(move || {
   316→        let reader = BufReader::with_capacity(4096, stdout);
   317→        let mut buf = Vec::new();
   318→        let mut r = reader;
   319→        loop {
   320→            buf.clear();
   321→            match r.read_until(b'\n', &mut buf) {
   322→                Ok(0) => break,
   323→                Ok(_) => {
   324→                    let s = String::from_utf8_lossy(&buf).to_string();
   325→                    if tx_out.send(s).is_err() {
   326→                        break;
   327→                    }
   328→                }
   329→                Err(_) => break,
   330→            }
   331→        }
   332→    });
   333→
   334→    // Stderr reader thread
   335→    std::thread::spawn(move || {
   336→        let reader = BufReader::with_capacity(4096, stderr);
   337→        let mut buf = Vec::new();
   338→        let mut r = reader;
   339→        loop {
   340→            buf.clear();
   341→            match r.read_until(b'\n', &mut buf) {
   342→                Ok(0) => break,
   343→                Ok(_) => {
   344→                    let s = String::from_utf8_lossy(&buf).to_string();
   345→                    if tx_err.send(s).is_err() {
   346→                        break;
   347→                    }
   348→                }
   349→                Err(_) => break,
   350→            }
   351→        }
   352→    });
   353→
   354→    // Drop our copy of tx so rx closes when threads finish
   355→    let mut output = String::new();
   356→    let mut timed_out = false;
   357→    let mut truncated = false;
   358→
   359→    loop {
   360→        if Instant::now() >= deadline {
   361→            let _ = child.kill();
   362→            timed_out = true;
   363→            break;
   364→        }
   365→
   366→        match rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(50)) {
   367→            Ok(chunk) => {
   368→                stream_cb(&chunk);
   369→                if output.len() + chunk.len() > BASH_OUTPUT_LIMIT {
   370→                    let remaining = BASH_OUTPUT_LIMIT.saturating_sub(output.len());
   371→                    output.push_str(&chunk[..chunk.floor_char_boundary(remaining)]);
   372→                    let _ = child.kill();
   373→                    truncated = true;
   374→                    break;
   375→                }
   376→                output.push_str(&chunk);
   377→            }
   378→            Err(mpsc::RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => {
   379→                // Check if process has exited
   380→                match child.try_wait() {
   381→                    Ok(Some(_)) => {
   382→                        // Process finished — drain remaining
   383→                        while let Ok(chunk) = rx.try_recv() {
   384→                            stream_cb(&chunk);
   385→                            if output.len() + chunk.len() <= BASH_OUTPUT_LIMIT {
   386→                                output.push_str(&chunk);
   387→                            } else if !truncated {
   388→                                let remaining = BASH_OUTPUT_LIMIT.saturating_sub(output.len());
   389→                                output.push_str(&chunk[..chunk.floor_char_boundary(remaining)]);
   390→                                truncated = true;
   391→                            }
   392→                        }
   393→                        break;
   394→                    }
   395→                    Ok(None) => continue,
   396→                    Err(_) => break,
   397→                }
   398→            }
   399→            Err(mpsc::RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => {
   400→                // Both threads done
   401→                let _ = child.wait();
   402→                break;
   403→            }
   404→        }
   405→    }
   406→
   407→    if truncated {
   408→        let _ = child.wait();
   409→        return Err(format!(
   410→            "Command output exceeded 1MB limit (truncated):\n{output}"
   411→        ));
   412→    }
   413→
   414→    if timed_out {
   415→        if output.is_empty() {
   416→            return Err(format!("Command timed out after 120s: {command}"));
   417→        }
   418→        return Err(format!(
   419→            "Command timed out after 120s (partial output):\n{output}"
   420→        ));
   421→    }
   422→
   423→    let status = child
   424→        .wait()
   425→        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to wait for process: {e}"))?;
   426→
   427→    if status.success() {
   428→        Ok(output)
   429→    } else {
   430→        let code = status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
   431→        if output.is_empty() {
   432→            Err(format!("Command failed with exit code {code}"))
   433→        } else {
   434→            Err(format!("Command failed with exit code {code}:\n{output}"))
   435→        }
   436→    }
   437→}
   438→
   439→fn edit_exec(input: &Value) -> Result<String, String> {
   440→    let file_path = input["file_path"]
   441→        .as_str()
   442→        .ok_or("Missing required parameter: file_path")?;
   443→    let old_str = input["old_str"]
   444→        .as_str()
   445→        .ok_or("Missing required parameter: old_str")?;
   446→    let new_str = input["new_str"]
   447→        .as_str()
   448→        .ok_or("Missing required parameter: new_str")?;
   449→    let replace_all = input["replace_all"].as_bool().unwrap_or(false);
   450→
   451→    let path = Path::new(file_path);
   452→
   453→    const EDIT_SIZE_LIMIT: u64 = 102_400;
   454→
   455→    // Empty old_str: create file (if missing) or append (if exists)
   456→    if old_str.is_empty() {
   457→        if path.exists() {
   458→            let metadata =
   459→                std::fs::metadata(path).map_err(|e| format!("Cannot read metadata: {e}"))?;
   460→            if metadata.len() > EDIT_SIZE_LIMIT {
   461→                return Err(format!(
   462→                    "File too large for edit: {} bytes (limit: 100KB)",
   463→                    metadata.len()
   464→                ));
   465→            }
   466→            // Append
   467→            let mut content =
   468→                std::fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| format!("Cannot read file: {e}"))?;
   469→            content.push_str(new_str);
   470→            std::fs::write(path, &content).map_err(|e| format!("Cannot write file: {e}"))?;
   471→            return Ok(format!("Appended to {file_path}"));
   472→        } else {
   473→            if new_str.len() as u64 > EDIT_SIZE_LIMIT {
   474→                return Err(format!(
   475→                    "Content too large for create: {} bytes (limit: 100KB)",
   476→                    new_str.len()
   477→                ));
   478→            }
   479→            // Create with parent dirs
   480→            if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
   481→                std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)
   482→                    .map_err(|e| format!("Cannot create directories: {e}"))?;
   483→            }
   484→            std::fs::write(path, new_str).map_err(|e| format!("Cannot create file: {e}"))?;
   485→            return Ok(format!("Created {file_path}"));
   486→        }
   487→    }
   488→
   489→    // Normal edit: read, find, replace
   490→    let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| format!("Cannot read file: {e}"))?;
   491→
   492→    let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path).map_err(|e| format!("Cannot read metadata: {e}"))?;
   493→    if metadata.len() > EDIT_SIZE_LIMIT {
   494→        return Err(format!(
   495→            "File too large for edit: {} bytes (limit: 100KB)",
   496→            metadata.len()
   497→        ));
   498→    }
   499→
   500→    if replace_all {
   501→        if !content.contains(old_str) {
   502→            return Err(format!("Text not found in {file_path}"));
   503→        }
   504→        let new_content = content.replace(old_str, new_str);
   505→        let count = content.matches(old_str).count();
   506→        std::fs::write(path, &new_content).map_err(|e| format!("Cannot write file: {e}"))?;
   507→        return Ok(format!("Replaced {count} occurrences in {file_path}"));
   508→    }
   509→
   510→    // Single exact match
   511→    let count = content.matches(old_str).count();
   512→    if count == 0 {
   513→        return Err(format!("Text not found in {file_path}"));
   514→    }
   515→    if count > 1 {
   516→        return Err(format!(
   517→            "Found {count} matches in {file_path} (expected exactly 1). Use replace_all=true for bulk replacement."
   518→        ));
   519→    }
   520→
   521→    let new_content = content.replacen(old_str, new_str, 1);
   522→    std::fs::write(path, &new_content).map_err(|e| format!("Cannot write file: {e}"))?;
   523→
   524→    Ok(format!("Edited {file_path}"))
   525→}
   526→
   527→fn grep_exec(input: &Value) -> Result<String, String> {
   528→    let pattern = input["pattern"]
   529→        .as_str()
   530→        .ok_or("Missing required parameter: pattern")?;
   531→    let path = input["path"].as_str().unwrap_or(".");
   532→    let file_type = input["file_type"].as_str();
   533→    let case_sensitive = input["case_sensitive"].as_bool().unwrap_or(true);
   534→
   535→    // Check rg is installed (cached — runs `which` once per process)
   536→    static RG_AVAILABLE: OnceLock<bool> = OnceLock::new();
   537→    let rg_ok = *RG_AVAILABLE.get_or_init(|| {
   538→        Command::new("which")
   539→            .arg("rg")
   540→            .output()
   541→            .map(|o| o.status.success())
   542→            .unwrap_or(false)
   543→    });
   544→    if !rg_ok {
   545→        return Err(
   546→            "ripgrep (rg) is not installed. Install it with: brew install ripgrep (macOS) or apt install ripgrep (Linux)"
   547→                .to_string(),
   548→        );
   549→    }
   550→
   551→    let mut cmd = Command::new("rg");
   552→    cmd.arg("--max-count=50")
   553→        .arg("--line-number")
   554→        .arg("--no-heading")
   555→        .arg("--color=never");
   556→
   557→    if !case_sensitive {
   558→        cmd.arg("-i");
   559→    }
   560→
   561→    if let Some(ft) = file_type {
   562→        cmd.arg("--type").arg(ft);
   563→    }
   564→
   565→    cmd.arg(pattern).arg(path);
   566→
   567→    let output = cmd.output().map_err(|e| format!("Failed to run rg: {e}"))?;
   568→
   569→    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string();
   570→    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string();
   571→
   572→    if !output.status.success() {
   573→        let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
   574→        if code == 1 {
   575→            // rg exit 1 = no matches
   576→            return Ok("No matches found".to_string());
   577→        }
   578→        if !stderr.is_empty() {
   579→            return Err(stderr.trim().to_string());
   580→        }
   581→        return Err(format!("rg exited with code {code}"));
   582→    }
   583→
   584→    let result = stdout.trim().to_string();
   585→    if result.is_empty() {
   586→        Ok("No matches found".to_string())
   587→    } else {
   588→        Ok(result)
   589→    }
   590→}

   892→        let source = std::fs::read_to_string("src/tools/mod.rs").unwrap();
   893→        // Find the glob_exec function body
   894→        let glob_start = source.find("fn glob_exec").unwrap();
   895→        let glob_end = source[glob_start..]
   896→            .find("\nfn ")
   897→            .unwrap_or(source.len() - glob_start);
   898→        let glob_body = &source[glob_start..glob_start + glob_end];
   899→        assert!(
   900→            !glob_body.contains("Command::new(\"bash\")"),
   901→            "glob_exec must not spawn bash"
   902→        );
   903→    }

     6→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
     7→pub enum AgentError {
     8→    #[error("API error: {0}")]
     9→    Api(#[from] reqwest::Error),
    10→
    11→    #[error("JSON error: {0}")]
    12→    Json(#[from] serde_json::Error),
    13→
    14→    #[error("HTTP {status}: {body}")]
    15→    HttpError {
    16→        status: u16,
    17→        retry_after: Option<u64>,
    18→        body: String,
    19→    },
    20→
    21→    #[error("Stream error (transient): {0}")]
    22→    StreamTransient(String),
    23→
    24→    #[error("Stream parse error: {0}")]
    25→    StreamParse(String),
    26→}
    27→
    28→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq)]
    29→pub enum ErrorClass {
    30→    Transient,
    31→    Permanent,
    32→}
    33→
    34→pub fn classify_error(e: &AgentError) -> ErrorClass {
    35→    match e {
    36→        AgentError::HttpError { status, .. } => match *status {
    37→            429 | 503 | 529 => ErrorClass::Transient,
    38→            s if s >= 500 => ErrorClass::Transient,
    39→            _ => ErrorClass::Permanent,
    40→        },
    41→        AgentError::StreamTransient(_) => ErrorClass::Transient,
    42→        AgentError::StreamParse(_) => ErrorClass::Permanent,
    43→        AgentError::Api(e) => {
    44→            if e.is_timeout() || e.is_connect() {
    45→                ErrorClass::Transient
    46→            } else {
    47→                ErrorClass::Permanent
    48→            }
    49→        }
    50→        AgentError::Json(_) => ErrorClass::Permanent,
    51→    }
    52→}

    56→    #[serde(rename = "end_turn")]

    59→    MaxTokens,
    60→    #[serde(rename = "tool_use")]
    61→    ToolUse,
    62→}

    64→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    65→#[serde(tag = "type")]
    66→pub enum ContentBlock {
    67→    #[serde(rename = "text")]
    68→    Text { text: String },
    69→    #[serde(rename = "tool_use")]
    70→    ToolUse {
    71→        id: String,

    74→    },
    75→    #[serde(rename = "tool_result")]
    76→    ToolResult {
    77→        tool_use_id: String,

    79→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    80→        is_error: Option<bool>,
    81→    },
    82→}

    90→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    91→pub struct Usage {
    92→    pub input_tokens: u64,

    97→
    98→pub struct AnthropicClient {
    99→    client: Client,
   100→    api_url: String,
   101→    api_key: Option<String>,
   102→}
   103→
   104→impl AnthropicClient {
   105→    pub fn new(api_url: &str) -> Self {
   106→        let client = Client::builder()
   107→            .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))
   108→            .timeout(Duration::from_secs(300))
   109→            .build()
   110→            .expect("failed to build HTTP client");
   111→
   112→        let api_key = std::env::var("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY").ok();
   113→
   114→        Self {
   115→            client,
   116→            api_url: api_url.trim_end_matches('/').to_string(),
   117→            api_key,
   118→        }
   119→    }
   120→
   121→    pub fn api_url(&self) -> &str {
   122→        &self.api_url
   123→    }
   124→
   125→    pub fn has_api_key(&self) -> bool {
   126→        self.api_key.is_some()
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    pub async fn send_message(
   130→        &self,
   131→        model: &str,
   132→        max_tokens: u32,
   133→        system: &str,
   134→        messages: &[Message],
   135→        tools: &[serde_json::Value],
   136→        stream_callback: &mut dyn FnMut(&str),
   137→    ) -> Result<(Vec<ContentBlock>, StopReason, Usage), AgentError> {
   138→        let url = format!("{}/v1/messages", self.api_url);
   139→
   140→        let mut body = serde_json::json!({
   141→            "model": model,
   142→            "max_tokens": max_tokens,
   143→            "system": [{
   144→                "type": "text",
   145→                "text": system,
   146→                "cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral"}
   147→            }],
   148→            "messages": messages,
   149→            "stream": true,
   150→        });
   151→
   152→        if !tools.is_empty() {
   153→            let mut cached_tools = tools.to_vec();
   154→            if let Some(last) = cached_tools.last_mut() {
   155→                last["cache_control"] = serde_json::json!({"type": "ephemeral"});
   156→            }
   157→            body["tools"] = serde_json::Value::Array(cached_tools);
   158→        }
   159→
   160→        let mut req = self
   161→            .client
   162→            .post(&url)
   163→            .header("anthropic-version", "2023-06-01");
   164→
   165→        if let Some(ref key) = self.api_key {
   166→            req = req.header("x-api-key", key);
   167→        }
   168→
   169→        let resp: reqwest::Response = req.json(&body).send().await?;
   170→
   171→        let status = resp.status();
   172→        if !status.is_success() {
   173→            let retry_after: Option<u64> = resp
   174→                .headers()
   175→                .get("retry-after")
   176→                .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
   177→                .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());
   178→            let body_text = resp.text().await.unwrap_or_default();
   179→            return Err(AgentError::HttpError {
   180→                status: status.as_u16(),
   181→                retry_after,
   182→                body: body_text,
   183→            });
   184→        }
   185→
   186→        let stream = resp.bytes_stream();
   187→        parse_sse_stream(stream, stream_callback).await
   188→    }
   189→}
   190→
   191→/// Parse SSE stream into content blocks, stop reason, and usage.
   192→///
   193→/// We collect content_block_start events to initialize blocks, then
   194→/// content_block_delta events to append text or accumulate tool input JSON,
   195→/// message_start for input usage, and message_delta for stop_reason + output usage.
   196→async fn parse_sse_stream<S>(
   197→    stream: S,
   198→    callback: &mut dyn FnMut(&str),
   199→) -> Result<(Vec<ContentBlock>, StopReason, Usage), AgentError>
   200→where
   201→    S: futures_util::Stream<Item = Result<bytes::Bytes, reqwest::Error>> + Unpin,
   202→{
   203→    let mut content_blocks: Vec<ContentBlock> = Vec::new();
   204→    let mut stop_reason: Option<StopReason> = None;
   205→    let mut usage = Usage::default();
   206→    let mut buffer = String::new();
   207→
   208→    // Track in-progress tool_use input JSON accumulation per block index
   209→    let mut tool_input_bufs: std::collections::HashMap<usize, String> =
   210→        std::collections::HashMap::new();
   211→
   212→    futures_util::pin_mut!(stream);
   213→
   214→    while let Some(chunk_result) = stream.next().await {
   215→        let chunk = chunk_result.map_err(|e| AgentError::StreamTransient(e.to_string()))?;
   216→        buffer.push_str(&String::from_utf8_lossy(&chunk));
   217→
   218→        // Process complete SSE lines
   219→        while let Some(pos) = buffer.find("\n\n") {
   220→            {
   221→                let event_block = &buffer[..pos];
   222→
   223→                for line in event_block.lines() {
   224→                    if let Some(data) = line.strip_prefix("data: ") {
   225→                        if data == "[DONE]" {
   226→                            continue;
   227→                        }
   228→
   229→                        let parsed: serde_json::Value = match serde_json::from_str(data) {
   230→                            Ok(v) => v,
   231→                            Err(_) => continue,
   232→                        };
   233→
   234→                        let event_type = parsed["type"].as_str().unwrap_or("");
   235→
   236→                        match event_type {
   237→                            "message_start" => {
   238→                                if let Some(u) = parsed.get("message").and_then(|m| m.get("usage"))
   239→                                {
   240→                                    usage.input_tokens = u["input_tokens"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0);
   241→                                    usage.cache_creation_input_tokens =
   242→                                        u["cache_creation_input_tokens"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0);
   243→                                    usage.cache_read_input_tokens =
   244→                                        u["cache_read_input_tokens"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0);
   245→                                }
   246→                            }
   247→                            "content_block_start" => {
   248→                                let cb = &parsed["content_block"];
   249→                                match cb["type"].as_str() {
   250→                                    Some("text") => {
   251→                                        content_blocks.push(ContentBlock::Text {
   252→                                            text: String::new(),
   253→                                        });
   254→                                    }
   255→                                    Some("tool_use") => {
   256→                                        let idx = content_blocks.len();
   257→                                        content_blocks.push(ContentBlock::ToolUse {
   258→                                        id: cb["id"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string(),
   259→                                        name: cb["name"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string(),
   260→                                        input: serde_json::Value::Object(serde_json::Map::new()),
   261→                                    });
   262→                                        tool_input_bufs.insert(idx, String::new());
   263→                                    }
   264→                                    _ => {}
   265→                                }
   266→                            }
   267→                            "content_block_delta" => {
   268→                                let index = parsed["index"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0) as usize;
   269→                                let delta = &parsed["delta"];
   270→
   271→                                match delta["type"].as_str() {
   272→                                    Some("text_delta") => {
   273→                                        if let Some(text) = delta["text"].as_str() {
   274→                                            callback(text);
   275→                                            if let Some(ContentBlock::Text { text: ref mut t }) =
   276→                                                content_blocks.get_mut(index)
   277→                                            {
   278→                                                t.push_str(text);
   279→                                            }
   280→                                        }
   281→                                    }
   282→                                    Some("input_json_delta") => {
   283→                                        if let Some(partial) = delta["partial_json"].as_str() {
   284→                                            if let Some(buf) = tool_input_bufs.get_mut(&index) {
   285→                                                buf.push_str(partial);
   286→                                            }
   287→                                        }
   288→                                    }
   289→                                    _ => {}
   290→                                }
   291→                            }
   292→                            "content_block_stop" => {
   293→                                let index = parsed["index"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0) as usize;
   294→                                // Finalize tool_use input JSON
   295→                                if let Some(json_str) = tool_input_bufs.remove(&index) {
   296→                                    if let Some(ContentBlock::ToolUse { ref mut input, .. }) =
   297→                                        content_blocks.get_mut(index)
   298→                                    {
   299→                                        match serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&json_str) {
   300→                                            Ok(v) => *input = v,
   301→                                            Err(e) => {
   302→                                                eprintln!(
   303→                                                    "[error] Failed to parse tool input JSON \
   304→                                                     for block {index}: {e}"
   305→                                                );
   306→                                                // Set to Null so run_pre_dispatch's null-input
   307→                                                // check catches it and produces a clean error
   308→                                                *input = serde_json::Value::Null;
   309→                                            }
   310→                                        }
   311→                                    }
   312→                                }
   313→                            }
   314→                            "message_delta" => {
   315→                                if let Some(sr) = parsed["delta"]["stop_reason"].as_str() {
   316→                                    stop_reason = match sr {
   317→                                        "end_turn" => Some(StopReason::EndTurn),
   318→                                        "max_tokens" => Some(StopReason::MaxTokens),
   319→                                        "tool_use" => Some(StopReason::ToolUse),
   320→                                        _ => None,
   321→                                    };
   322→                                }
   323→                                if let Some(u) = parsed.get("usage") {
   324→                                    usage.output_tokens = u["output_tokens"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0);
   325→                                }
   326→                            }
   327→                            "error" => {
   328→                                let err_type =
   329→                                    parsed["error"]["type"].as_str().unwrap_or("unknown");
   330→                                let err_msg = parsed["error"]["message"]
   331→                                    .as_str()
   332→                                    .unwrap_or("unknown error");
   333→                                match err_type {
   334→                                    "overloaded_error" | "api_error" | "rate_limit_error" => {
   335→                                        return Err(AgentError::StreamTransient(format!(
   336→                                            "{err_type}: {err_msg}"
   337→                                        )));
   338→                                    }
   339→                                    _ => {
   340→                                        return Err(AgentError::StreamParse(format!(
   341→                                            "{err_type}: {err_msg}"
   342→                                        )));
   343→                                    }
   344→                                }
   345→                            }
   346→                            _ => {}
   347→                        }
   348→                    }
   349→                }
   350→            }
   351→            buffer.drain(..pos + 2);
   352→        }
   353→    }
   354→
   355→    let stop = stop_reason.ok_or_else(|| {
   356→        AgentError::StreamTransient(
   357→            "stream ended without stop_reason (connection drop)".to_string(),
   358→        )
   359→    })?;
   360→
   361→    Ok((content_blocks, stop, usage))
   362→}

    26→    26→#[derive(Debug, PartialEq)]
    27→    27→enum InstructionsResult {
    28→    28→    Found { filename: String, contents: String },
    29→    29→    Skipped { filename: String, reason: String },
    30→    30→    NotFound,
    31→    31→}
    32→    32→
    33→    33→/// Search cwd for CLAUDE.md then AGENTS.md. First match wins.
    34→    34→/// Returns Found on success, Skipped if a candidate exists but can't be loaded
    35→    35→/// (size limit, permissions), or NotFound if neither file exists.
    36→    36→fn load_project_instructions() -> InstructionsResult {
    37→    37→    let candidates = ["CLAUDE.md", "AGENTS.md"];
    38→    38→    let mut last_skipped: Option<InstructionsResult> = None;
    39→    39→
    40→    40→    for candidate in &candidates {
    41→    41→        let metadata = match std::fs::metadata(candidate) {
    42→    42→            Ok(m) => m,
    43→    43→            Err(_) => continue, // file not found or dangling symlink
    44→    44→        };
    45→    45→
    46→    46→        if metadata.len() as usize > PROJECT_INSTRUCTIONS_MAX_BYTES {
    47→    47→            last_skipped = Some(InstructionsResult::Skipped {
    48→    48→                filename: candidate.to_string(),
    49→    49→                reason: "exceeds 32KB".to_string(),
    50→    50→            });
    51→    51→            continue;
    52→    52→        }
    53→    53→
    54→    54→        match std::fs::read_to_string(candidate) {
    55→    55→            Ok(contents) => {
    56→    56→                return InstructionsResult::Found {
    57→    57→                    filename: candidate.to_string(),
    58→    58→                    contents,
    59→    59→                };
    60→    60→            }
    61→    61→            Err(e) => {
    62→    62→                last_skipped = Some(InstructionsResult::Skipped {
    63→    63→                    filename: candidate.to_string(),
    64→    64→                    reason: e.to_string(),
    65→    65→                });
    66→    66→                continue;
    67→    67→            }
    68→    68→        }
    69→    69→    }
    70→    70→
    71→    71→    last_skipped.unwrap_or(InstructionsResult::NotFound)
    72→    72→}

   101→   101→fn build_system_prompt() -> String {
   102→   102→    let cwd = std::env::current_dir()
   103→   103→        .map(|p| p.display().to_string())
   104→   104→        .unwrap_or_else(|_| ".".to_string());
   105→   105→    let platform = std::env::consts::OS;
   106→   106→
   107→   107→    format!(
   108→   108→        "You are a coding assistant with access to tools for reading, searching, editing files, \
   109→   109→         and running commands.\n\n\
   110→   110→         Environment:\n\
   111→   111→         - Working directory: {cwd}\n\
   112→   112→         - Platform: {platform}\n\n\
   113→   113→         Available tools (use PascalCase names exactly):\n\
   114→   114→         - Read: Read file contents (max 1MB)\n\

   117→   117→         - Edit: Edit files with exact text replacement (max 100KB, use replace_all for bulk)\n\
   118→   118→         - Grep: Search file contents with ripgrep (max 50 matches)\n\n\
   119→   119→         Guidelines:\n\
   120→   120→         - Read files before editing them\n\
   121→   121→         - Use Grep to find code before making changes\n\
   122→   122→         - Prefer targeted edits over full file rewrites\n\

   126→   126→
   127→   127→/// Trim conversation at exchange boundaries to fit within context budget.
   128→   128→/// Preserves the first user message and trims from the front, keeping
   129→   129→/// tool_use/tool_result pairs together.
   130→   130→fn trim_conversation(messages: &mut Vec<Message>) {
   131→   131→    let size: usize = messages
   132→   132→        .iter()
   133→   133→        .map(|m| serde_json::to_string(m).unwrap_or_default().len())
   134→   134→        .sum();
   135→   135→
   136→   136→    if size <= CONTEXT_BUDGET_BYTES || messages.len() <= 2 {
   137→   137→        return;
   138→   138→    }
   139→   139→
   140→   140→    // Keep first message, trim from front of the rest
   141→   141→    let first = messages.remove(0);
   142→   142→    while messages.len() > 1 {
   143→   143→        let new_size: usize = std::iter::once(&first)
   144→   144→            .chain(messages.iter())
   145→   145→            .map(|m| serde_json::to_string(m).unwrap_or_default().len())
   146→   146→            .sum();
   147→   147→        if new_size <= CONTEXT_BUDGET_BYTES {
   148→   148→            break;
   149→   149→        }
   150→   150→        // Remove pairs to maintain alternation
   151→   151→        messages.remove(0);
   152→   152→        if !messages.is_empty() && messages[0].role == "assistant" {
   153→   153→            messages.remove(0);
   154→   154→        }
   155→   155→    }
   156→   156→    messages.insert(0, first);
   157→   157→}
   158→   158→
   159→   159→/// Gate trim_conversation on actual token usage from the API.
   160→   160→/// - last_input_tokens == 0: no data yet, run byte-based trim (safety net)
   161→   161→/// - last_input_tokens > 0 && < TRIM_THRESHOLD: skip trim (context is safe)
   162→   162→/// - last_input_tokens >= TRIM_THRESHOLD: run byte-based trim
   163→   163→fn trim_if_needed(messages: &mut Vec<Message>, last_input_tokens: u64) {
   164→   164→    if last_input_tokens == 0 || last_input_tokens >= TRIM_THRESHOLD {
   165→   165→        trim_conversation(messages);
   166→   166→    }
   167→   167→}
   168→   168→
   169→   169→/// Recover conversation alternation after API errors.
   170→   170→/// Pops trailing User message and any orphaned tool_use to maintain
   171→   171→/// the user/assistant alternation invariant.
   172→   172→fn recover_conversation(messages: &mut Vec<Message>) {
   173→   173→    // Guard: never empty the conversation entirely — at minimum keep the
   174→   174→    // first user message so the next API call has something to send.
   175→   175→    if messages.len() <= 1 {
   176→   176→        return;
   177→   177→    }
   178→   178→    // Pop trailing user message if present (but keep at least 1 message)
   179→   179→    if messages.len() > 1 {
   180→   180→        if let Some(last) = messages.last() {
   181→   181→            if last.role == "user" {
   182→   182→                messages.pop();
   183→   183→            }
   184→   184→        }
   185→   185→    }
   186→   186→    // Pop trailing assistant message that has only tool_use blocks (orphaned)
   187→   187→    if messages.len() > 1 {
   188→   188→        if let Some(last) = messages.last() {
   189→   189→            if last.role == "assistant" {
   190→   190→                let only_tool_use = last
   191→   191→                    .content
   192→   192→                    .iter()
   193→   193→                    .all(|b| matches!(b, ContentBlock::ToolUse { .. }));
   194→   194→                if only_tool_use {
   195→   195→                    messages.pop();
   196→   196→                    // Also pop the user message before it to maintain alternation
   197→   197→                    if messages.len() > 1 {
   198→   198→                        if let Some(last) = messages.last() {
   199→   199→                            if last.role == "user" {
   200→   200→                                messages.pop();
   201→   201→                            }
   202→   202→                        }
   203→   203→                    }
   204→   204→                }
   205→   205→            }
   206→   206→        }
   207→   207→    }
   208→   208→}

   210→   210→fn use_color() -> bool {

   213→   213→
   214→   214→/// Filter out null-input tool_use blocks from MaxTokens truncation.
   215→   215→/// Returns the filtered blocks. If all tool_use blocks had null input
   216→   216→/// and nothing remains, returns a vec with a placeholder text block.
   217→   217→fn filter_null_input_tool_use(blocks: Vec<ContentBlock>) -> Vec<ContentBlock> {
   218→   218→    let filtered: Vec<ContentBlock> = blocks
   219→   219→        .into_iter()
   220→   220→        .filter(|b| {
   221→   221→            if let ContentBlock::ToolUse { input, .. } = b {
   222→   222→                !input.is_null()
   223→   223→            } else {
   224→   224→                true
   225→   225→            }
   226→   226→        })
   227→   227→        .collect();
   228→   228→
   229→   229→    if filtered.is_empty() {
   230→   230→        vec![ContentBlock::Text {
   231→   231→            text: "[Response truncated]".to_string(),
   232→   232→        }]
   233→   233→    } else {
   234→   234→        filtered
   235→   235→    }
   236→   236→}
   237→   237→
   238→   238→/// Decision from pre-dispatch protocol: allow dispatch, block with a ToolResult, or trip threshold.
   239→   239→#[derive(Debug)]
   240→   240→enum PreDispatchResult {
   241→   241→    Allow,
   242→   242→    Blocked(ContentBlock),
   243→   243→    ThresholdTripped,
   244→   244→}
   245→   245→
   246→   246→/// Unified pre-dispatch protocol for both parallel and sequential paths.
   247→   247→/// Checks null-input, runs pre-hook, manages block counting and threshold checks.
   248→   248→/// Block counts are mutated in place so callers cannot forget to apply them.
   249→   249→async fn run_pre_dispatch(
   250→   250→    hooks: &HookRunner,
   251→   251→    id: &str,
   252→   252→    name: &str,
   253→   253→    input: &serde_json::Value,
   254→   254→    iterations: usize,
   255→   255→    consecutive_block_count: &mut usize,
   256→   256→    total_block_count: &mut usize,
   257→   257→) -> PreDispatchResult {
   258→   258→    // Null-input safety net (truncated tool_use from API anomaly)
   259→   259→    if input.is_null() {
   260→   260→        return PreDispatchResult::Blocked(ContentBlock::ToolResult {
   261→   261→            tool_use_id: id.to_string(),
   262→   262→            content: "null input (truncated tool_use)".to_string(),
   263→   263→            is_error: Some(true),
   264→   264→        });
   265→   265→    }
   266→   266→
   267→   267→    let pre_result = hooks.run_pre_tool_use(name, input, iterations).await;
   268→   268→
   269→   269→    match pre_result {
   270→   270→        PreToolResult::Block { reason, .. } => {
   271→   271→            *consecutive_block_count += 1;
   272→   272→            *total_block_count += 1;
   273→   273→
   274→   274→            if *consecutive_block_count >= MAX_CONSECUTIVE_BLOCKS {
   275→   275→                eprintln!("[hooks] Consecutive block limit ({MAX_CONSECUTIVE_BLOCKS}) reached");
   276→   276→                return PreDispatchResult::ThresholdTripped;
   277→   277→            }
   278→   278→            if *total_block_count >= MAX_TOTAL_BLOCKS {
   279→   279→                eprintln!("[hooks] Total block limit ({MAX_TOTAL_BLOCKS}) reached");
   280→   280→                return PreDispatchResult::ThresholdTripped;
   281→   281→            }
   282→   282→
   283→   283→            PreDispatchResult::Blocked(ContentBlock::ToolResult {
   284→   284→                tool_use_id: id.to_string(),
   285→   285→                content: reason,
   286→   286→                is_error: Some(true),
   287→   287→            })
   288→   288→        }
   289→   289→        PreToolResult::Allow => {
   290→   290→            *consecutive_block_count = 0;
   291→   291→            PreDispatchResult::Allow
   292→   292→        }
   293→   293→    }
   294→   294→}
   295→   295→
   296→   296→/// Unified post-dispatch protocol for both parallel and sequential paths.
   297→   297→/// Formats and logs the result, then runs post-hooks. Returns true if a
   298→   298→/// convergence signal was received (caller should set signal_break).
   299→   299→async fn run_post_dispatch(
   300→   300→    hooks: &HookRunner,
   301→   301→    name: &str,
   302→   302→    input: &serde_json::Value,
   303→   303→    content: &str,
   304→   304→    is_error: bool,
   305→   305→    iterations: usize,
   306→   306→    verbose: bool,
   307→   307→) -> bool {
   308→   308→    let display = format_tool_result_display(content, is_error, verbose);
   309→   309→    eprintln!("{display}");
   310→   310→
   311→   311→    let post_result = hooks
   312→   312→        .run_post_tool_use(name, input, content, is_error, iterations)
   313→   313→        .await;
   314→   314→    matches!(post_result, PostToolResult::Signal { .. })
   315→   315→}
   316→   316→
   317→   317→fn log_tool_dispatch(name: &str, input: &serde_json::Value, verbose: bool) {
   318→   318→    if verbose {
   319→   319→        eprintln!("\n[tool] {name}({})", truncate_json(input, 100));
   320→   320→    } else {
   321→   321→        eprintln!("\n[tool] {name}");
   322→   322→    }
   323→   323→}
   324→   324→
   325→   325→fn threshold_reason_str(consecutive_block_count: usize) -> &'static str {
   326→   326→    if consecutive_block_count >= MAX_CONSECUTIVE_BLOCKS {
   327→   327→        "block_limit_consecutive"
   328→   328→    } else {
   329→   329→        "block_limit_total"
   330→   330→    }
   331→   331→}
   332→   332→
   333→   333→/// Dispatch a tool and wrap the result as a ContentBlock::ToolResult.
   334→   334→/// Used by the parallel path inside spawn_blocking.
   335→   335→fn dispatch_to_tool_result(id: String, name: String, input: serde_json::Value) -> ContentBlock {
   336→   336→    let result = dispatch_tool(&name, &input, &mut |_: &str| {});
   337→   337→    let (content, is_error) = match result {
   338→   338→        Ok(output) => (output, false),
   339→   339→        Err(err) => (err, true),
   340→   340→    };
   341→   341→    ContentBlock::ToolResult {
   342→   342→        tool_use_id: id,
   343→   343→        content,
   344→   344→        is_error: if is_error { Some(true) } else { None },
   345→   345→    }
   346→   346→}
   347→   347→
   348→   348→/// Join spawned parallel futures into their slots, handling panics.
   349→   349→async fn join_spawned_futures(
   350→   350→    futures: Vec<(usize, tokio::task::JoinHandle<ContentBlock>)>,
   351→   351→    slots: &mut [Option<ContentBlock>],
   352→   352→    tool_ids: &[(String, String, serde_json::Value)],
   353→   353→) {
   354→   354→    let handles: Vec<_> = futures
   355→   355→        .into_iter()
   356→   356→        .map(|(idx, h)| async move { (idx, h.await) })
   357→   357→        .collect();
   358→   358→    for (idx, result) in futures_util::future::join_all(handles).await {
   359→   359→        slots[idx] = Some(match result {
   360→   360→            Ok(block) => block,
   361→   361→            Err(_) => ContentBlock::ToolResult {
   362→   362→                tool_use_id: tool_ids[idx].0.clone(),
   363→   363→                content: "tool panicked".to_string(),
   364→   364→                is_error: Some(true),
   365→   365→            },
   366→   366→        });
   367→   367→    }
   368→   368→}
   369→   369→
   370→   370→fn format_tool_result_display(result: &str, is_error: bool, verbose: bool) -> String {
   371→   371→    if is_error {
   372→   372→        let preview = if result.len() > 200 {
   373→   373→            format!("{}...", &result[..result.floor_char_boundary(200)])
   374→   374→        } else {
   375→   375→            result.to_string()
   376→   376→        };
   377→   377→        format!("  Error: {preview}")
   378→   378→    } else if verbose {
   379→   379→        result.to_string()
   380→   380→    } else {
   381→   381→        format!("  ({} chars)", result.len())
   382→   382→    }
   383→   383→}

   873→   873→fn truncate_json(value: &serde_json::Value, max_len: usize) -> String {
   874→   874→    let s = value.to_string();
   875→   875→    if s.len() <= max_len {
   876→   876→        s
   877→   877→    } else {
   878→   878→        format!("{}...", &s[..s.floor_char_boundary(max_len)])
   879→   879→    }
   880→   880→}
   881→   881→
   882→   882→const MAX_CONTINUATIONS: usize = 3;
   883→   883→
   884→   884→/// Determine what to do after a MaxTokens stop_reason.
   885→   885→/// Returns the action to take given the filtered content blocks and current continuation count.
   886→   886→#[derive(Debug, PartialEq)]
   887→   887→enum MaxTokensAction {
   888→   888→    /// Response was empty (only placeholder) — break immediately
   889→   889→    BreakEmpty,
   890→   890→    /// Valid tool_use blocks present — fall through to tool dispatch
   891→   891→    DispatchTools,
   892→   892→    /// Text-only, under cap — inject continuation prompt
   893→   893→    Continue,
   894→   894→    /// Text-only, cap reached — break
   895→   895→    BreakCapReached,
   896→   896→}
   897→   897→
   898→   898→fn classify_max_tokens(blocks: &[ContentBlock], continuation_count: usize) -> MaxTokensAction {
   899→   899→    // Check for empty response (only the "[Response truncated]" placeholder)
   900→   900→    let is_empty = blocks.len() == 1
   901→   901→        && matches!(&blocks[0], ContentBlock::Text { text } if text == "[Response truncated]");
   902→   902→    if is_empty {
   903→   903→        return MaxTokensAction::BreakEmpty;
   904→   904→    }
   905→   905→
   906→   906→    let has_valid_tools = blocks
   907→   907→        .iter()
   908→   908→        .any(|b| matches!(b, ContentBlock::ToolUse { .. }));
   909→   909→
   910→   910→    if has_valid_tools {
   911→   911→        MaxTokensAction::DispatchTools
   912→   912→    } else if continuation_count < MAX_CONTINUATIONS {
   913→   913→        MaxTokensAction::Continue
   914→   914→    } else {
   915→   915→        MaxTokensAction::BreakCapReached
   916→   916→    }
   917→   917→}

   919→   919→/// Check if stdin is a terminal (not piped).
   920→   920→fn atty_check() -> bool {
   921→   921→    std::io::IsTerminal::is_terminal(&io::stdin())
   922→   922→}

/// Maximum output size for bash commands (1MB). Commands that exceed this are
/// killed to prevent unbounded memory growth from runaway processes.
const BASH_OUTPUT_LIMIT: usize = 1_048_576;

fn bash_exec(input: &Value, stream_cb: &mut dyn FnMut(&str)) -> Result<String, String> {
    let command = input["command"]
        .as_str()
        .ok_or("Missing required parameter: command")?;

    if is_denied_command(command) {
        return Err(format!("Command blocked by safety guard: {command}"));
    }

    let timeout = Duration::from_secs(120);
    let deadline = Instant::now() + timeout;

    let mut child = Command::new("bash")
        .arg("-c")
        .arg(command)
        .stdout(Stdio::piped())
        .stderr(Stdio::piped())
        .spawn()
        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to spawn bash: {e}"))?;

    let stdout = child.stdout.take().unwrap();
    let stderr = child.stderr.take().unwrap();

    let (tx_out, rx) = mpsc::channel::<String>();
    let tx_err = tx_out.clone();

    // Stdout reader thread
    std::thread::spawn(move || {

   882→const MAX_CONTINUATIONS: usize = 3;
   883→
   884→/// Determine what to do after a MaxTokens stop_reason.
   885→/// Returns the action to take given the filtered content blocks and current continuation count.
   886→#[derive(Debug, PartialEq)]
   887→enum MaxTokensAction {
   888→    /// Response was empty (only placeholder) — break immediately
   889→    BreakEmpty,
   890→    /// Valid tool_use blocks present — fall through to tool dispatch
   891→    DispatchTools,
   892→    /// Text-only, under cap — inject continuation prompt
   893→    Continue,
   894→    /// Text-only, cap reached — break
   895→    BreakCapReached,
   896→}
   897→
   898→fn classify_max_tokens(blocks: &[ContentBlock], continuation_count: usize) -> MaxTokensAction {
   899→    // Check for empty response (only the "[Response truncated]" placeholder)
   900→    let is_empty = blocks.len() == 1
   901→        && matches!(&blocks[0], ContentBlock::Text { text } if text == "[Response truncated]");
   902→    if is_empty {
   903→        return MaxTokensAction::BreakEmpty;
   904→    }
   905→
   906→    let has_valid_tools = blocks
   907→        .iter()
   908→        .any(|b| matches!(b, ContentBlock::ToolUse { .. }));
   909→
   910→    if has_valid_tools {
   911→        MaxTokensAction::DispatchTools
   912→    } else if continuation_count < MAX_CONTINUATIONS {
   913→        MaxTokensAction::Continue
   914→    } else {
   915→        MaxTokensAction::BreakCapReached
   916→    }
   917→}
